spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates the feature's general idea but doesn't surface the named spec API symbols directly.

interactive.rationale: Interactive controls update compositor-cost output and a live probe checks WebXR Layers globals.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Page covers the promises the feature index makes for this concept.

edge_cases.rationale: No visible edge-case handling — only the success path is demonstrated.

references.rationale: Cites the verified chromestatus.com/feature/6634466544058368 reference link as required.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex, button overrides keep bg+color paired.

openQuestions.title: Surface more named spec API for webxr-layers
openQuestions.detail: The spec summary lists API surfaces this concept doesn't currently name on-page. Either rename the controls / readouts to mention them, or add a second concept page that targets the missing surface directly. The reviewer extracted these from the spec: WebXR Layers level 1. Layer types: XRProjectionLayer, XRQuadLayer, XRCylinderLayer, XREquirectLayer, XRCubeLayer. XRWebGLBinding factory methods. XRLayerLayout: mono / stereo / stereo-left-right / stereo-top-bottom. XRLayerQuality: text-optimized / graphics-optimized. Fixed foveation (0-1) on projection layers. nativeProjectionScaleFactor for resolution. XRWebGLSubImage with color, depth/stencil, ...

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one failure mode
openQuestions.detail: The page currently shows the happy path. Add a UI affordance that drives the feature into a failure mode the spec explicitly calls out (timeout, denied permission, cross-origin block, malformed input) and surface what the browser actually does.

summary: Workable concept with a couple of gaps. 2 open question(s) queued.
